compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
FIELD FINDING (plan-review claim gate non-convergence) — captured while executing this epic under /rebar-implement, 2026-07-12. Attempting to earn plan-review attestations for the 10 child leaves reproduced, at scale, the exact pathology this epic targets:

1. VERDICT INSTABILITY on identical plans: 74d9 and a8e5 each returned INDETERMINATE (0 blocking) on one round, then BLOCK (1 and 3 blocking respectively) on the very next round with the plan text UNCHANGED (only re-run). This is the >=32% same-material verdict instability from bug 5e40 (story a8e5's motivator), observed directly on the claim gate.

2. NON-MONOTONIC / NON-CONVERGENT remediation: block counts oscillate rather than decrease as valid findings are remediated. b9c0: 7 -> 4 -> 4 -> 6 blocks across 4 rounds (each round I fixed the named findings). 74d9: 1 -> 6 -> 2 -> INDETERMINATE -> 1. 9f25: 4 -> 1 -> 3. Each remediation that adds specificity mints new finer-grained findings (the ~95%-of-edits-mint-new-findings effect the epic Context cites), and verdict noise moves the count both ways.

3. NEW-CODE GROUNDING GAP: codebase-grounding criteria (G1G2/E4) abstain or return INDETERMINATE for plans that reference not-yet-written code (a new scorer, a new binary, a new field) — which is inherent to planning NEW work. This is structurally distinct from advisory churn and is a candidate for the a37b/a8e5 grounding hardening.

4. EXHAUSTIVE-BLAST-RADIUS TAIL: for b9c0's config flip, the gate enumerated the breaking pinning tests ONE per round (test_impact_model_versioning -> test_code_review_ws2:64 -> ws2:39/52 -> ...), rather than all at once, forcing a round per discovered test.

All blocks encountered were, individually, VALID and sharp (real spec gaps, false premises, missing edit targets, tautological tests, schema mismatches) — the gate's precision is good. The problem is CONVERGENCE + STABILITY at the aggregate/claim level: a fail-closed gate that does not monotonically converge cannot be satisfied by honest iterative remediation within a bounded number of rounds, especially for complex, dependency-coupled leaves (b9c0's correct threshold genuinely depends on sibling 9f25's not-yet-performed derivation). This is direct evidence for the epic's Success Criteria (code-review looseness is one half; plan-review over-tightness/instability is the mirror) and belongs in the epic's measurement note.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
FIELD FINDING — REFINEMENT (supersedes the 'non-convergent gate' framing in the prior comment). After the operator directed a root-cause pass, the evidence substantially revises that conclusion:

1. The novelty rising floor + remediation_mode ARE active on origin/main (remediation_mode=True, novelty_drop_active=True defaults; re-reviews emitted REVIEW_RESULT sidecars and dropped 1/3/9 low-priority novel findings). It targets low-priority (<0.4) advisory churn and correctly does NOT floor high-impact (0.85-1.0) blocking findings — so it is not, and should not be, the lever for blocking convergence.

2. The blocking findings were VALID and code-accurate — the gate's precision was high throughout. What looked like 'non-convergence / verdict oscillation' was mostly MY remediations introducing NEW factual inaccuracies each round, which the gate correctly caught: I invented a non-existent eval field (scorer_args), misidentified a DET detector (there is no P6 sibling-overlap detector; P5 already emits subjects), over-engineered new context-plumbing for G7 when the ticket's intent was to read the parent via the always-available show_ticket tool, and mislabeled G7 CODEBASE_GROUNDED when tooling is granted by agentic execution_mode. EVERY leaf converged to PASS the moment its plan was corrected AGAINST THE ACTUAL SOURCE rather than against my assumptions — a8e5, 74d9, 9f25, b9c0 all passed on the code-grounded revision.

3. Genuine residual frictions (smaller than first claimed, still real for the measurement note): (a) verdict INSTABILITY at the margin (74d9/a8e5 flipped INDETERMINATE<->BLOCK on unchanged plans once each) — real but not the dominant effect; (b) NEW-CODE grounding INDETERMINATE for plans referencing not-yet-written code; (c) exhaustive test-blast-radius enumerated one pinning test per round rather than all at once.

REVISED TAKEAWAY for calibration: the plan-review claim gate is high-precision and effectively convergent WHEN the plan is made factually accurate against the codebase; the expensive rounds here were driven by plan inaccuracy (agent-side), not gate over-tightness. The mirror of the code-review looseness this epic fixes is NOT plan-review over-tightness — it is plan-review's correct insistence on code-accurate plans, which is costly for large new-code plans but not wrong. 10/11 tickets earned a genuine signed PASS with no --force.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: EPIC STATUS 2026-07-12 (/rebar-implement, end of session 1). CLAIM PHASE COMPLETE: all 11 tickets (epic + 10 leaves) signed through the plan-review claim gate, zero --force. Two calibration findings recorded (comments #1 + #2, the latter the refined/authoritative one). IMPLEMENTATION: 8/10 leaves implemented + committed locally on branch epic/9d50-calibration-followup (8-commit stack, HEAD 571d2cf37, tree clean), all green through pre-commit lint+typecheck: ac0e(0fb8d17e6), e1f2(2e99d189e), 74d9(ba58b34de), 24ec(94d6f3ce6), f32e(4707cd54e), a37b(7cd3a572e), 9f25(37a486899), b9c0(571d2cf37). NOT YET DONE: (a) implement a8e5 + d4cf (the 2 largest kernel leaves; plans hardened, see their status comments); (b) LANDING — nothing pushed to Gerrit, no leaves closed. Landing = push the full stack to gerrit HEAD:refs/for/main, iterate LLM-Review + Verified, close each leaf bottom-up after its Verified +1 (completion-verifier gate), then AC2: record the measurement-framework comment on THIS epic (baseline 0-blocks/162 + the re-runnable queries + link to deferred ticket 72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d which is parked in idea), then close the epic. Full handoff in session_log sparse-warm-tench (deb4-70c2-e647-43b8). NOTE: 9f25's 0.54 threshold is provisional (circular first-pass adjudication) — b9c0's AC records that a held-out adjudication must confirm before relying on the block in production.

compiled_state.description:
## Context
The 2026-07-12 calibration analysis (session logs remarkable-hydrated-nightjar / tainted-continuing-queensnake / shaggy-linoleum-gavial) found: (1) code review is too loose — 0 'BLOCK — finding' votes across 162 post-recalibration changes while confirmed must-fix findings sailed through as advisories (change #529's untested-degradation advisory scored impact 0.0 and went red on main 57 minutes after merge; #518's importlib code-execution findings at 0.54; #539's correctness findings at 0.60 — against a 0.95 threshold no real finding ever reached); (2) plan-review advisory load is protocol-inflated — CLAUDE.md mandates remediating ALL valid advisories (~13/round, 95% of remediation edits mint new findings), and with the convergence floors now default-ON (change 562) we want to observe organic agent behavior instead of mandated remediation; (3) completion-verifier defects are structural, not admissibility: FAIL verdicts persist nowhere, FAILs ratchet piecemeal (3b4e took 2 extra CI cycles), and 'children closed (signed)' epic ACs defeat the certifiable:false escape hatch (bf50/5f39 contagion, bug 02a3); and (4) leaf plans DRIFT from their parent's plan as leaves are claimed and edited — by epic close a leaf's ACs can describe a materially different deliverable than the parent declared, and no plan-review criterion checks a leaf's scope against its PARENT (drift is caught only at epic-close completion verification, far too late). The review that gates the CLAIM is the right moment to catch drift ticket-by-ticket. This 4th finding motivates child d4cf (the new G7 leaf-plan parent-containment criterion).

## Definitions & Sequencing
- **"serious-tier" (Success Criterion 1):** the code-review criteria whose findings map to the SERIOUS (0.9) consequence binaries in `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py` — prod-lane `data_loss_without_recovery`, `security_bypass_not_enforced_elsewhere`, `silent_wrong_feeding_a_decision`; maint-lane `unversioned_published_contract_break`, `safety_net_removal_without_replacement` — plus the `security` criterion. Child b9c0 enumerates the exact enabled set and pins it in a test; everything else stays advisory at 0.95.
- **code-v2 vs code-v3 cohort ordering (f32e vs 9f25/b9c0):** 9f25 derives, and b9c0 enables, a block threshold over the existing **code-v2** finding cohort — valid for code-v2 sidecars only. f32e adds a new maint binary and bumps `IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION` to **code-v3**, which per ADR-0036 starts a FRESH calibration cohort. Re-deriving/enabling any threshold over code-v3 is explicitly a FUTURE follow-up, NOT part of b9c0 — no threshold in this epic consumes code-v3. The two workstreams are independent by design; there is no ordering dependency between f32e and 9f25/b9c0, and b9c0's threshold governs code-v2 findings regardless of f32e's landing order.

## Success Criteria
- Code-review blocking is derived from adjudicated field data (the crossover threshold where a finding is more likely correctly-blocking than false-blocking), enabled for the serious-tier criteria (defined above), and passes the ADR-0036 A/B gate before flipping.
- Agent guidance no longer MANDATES advisory remediation (loosened to judgment, NOT reversed), so post-change review rounds measure organic advisory uptake.
- Completion-verifier FAILs are durably persisted and enumerate all unmet criteria in one verdict; the signed-children AC anti-pattern is documented.
- A plan-review criterion gates leaf-plan/parent-plan scope CONTAINMENT (a leaf's scope must be a subset of its parent's; the parent wins on conflict), firing on the claim-gating review so leaf drift is caught ticket-by-ticket instead of at epic close — shipped advisory per the ADR-0036 expand-contract pattern with fire-rate observable from day one (child d4cf).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All child tickets (the epic's work items) are closed, each with its change landed on main.
- [ ] An in-session measurement-framework artifact is recorded as a comment on this epic: (a) the pre-change BASELINE from the 2026-07-12 analysis (0 finding-blocks / 162 changes; ~13 advisories/round), (b) the exact re-runnable queries/commands for post-change measurement — code-review block/false-block counts from the `REVIEW_RESULT` (code_review_result_v1) sidecars, and plan-review advisory-uptake via `norm_id` survival across rounds — and (c) a pointer to where the ACTUAL post-deployment field data will be recorded: the committed, CI-verifiable file `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/g7-promotion-path.md` (G7 fire-rate + promotion band) plus this AC2 comment's re-runnable queries. (This is created at merge time and IS verifiable in-session; the actual multi-day telemetry is explicitly deferred, below.)
- [ ] The deferred post-deployment measurement is DOCUMENTED (this epic's AC2 measurement-framework comment + the committed `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/g7-promotion-path.md`) and is picked up NATURALLY by the next calibration session — which re-runs those queries against post-deploy field data, exactly as the 2026-07-12 session analyzed the prior cohort. No standalone tracking ticket is retained: operator decision 2026-07-12, the interim idea ticket 72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d was closed as handled-by-process (the one substantive follow-up — b9c0's provisional 0.54 threshold needing a non-circular held-out adjudication — is preserved in b9c0's AC + the AC2 caveat). That telemetry is OUT of this epic's close path: this epic closes on the measurement FRAMEWORK being in place, not on multi-day field data existing.
compiled_state.title: Review-gate calibration 2026-07-12: code-review blocking lane + advisory-guidance loosening + completion-verifier fixes
